name: query-builder
description: Interactive database query builder for generating optimized SQL and NoSQL queries.
capabilities: Generates SQL queries for PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, and SQL Server
Creates NoSQL queries for MongoDB, DynamoDB, Redis, and Cassandra
Writes ORM queries using Prisma, TypeORM, SQLAlchemy, and Mongoose
Explains query execution and performance considerations
Provides parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection
author: CuriousLearner
securitySummary: Pure prompt-based skill containing only markdown documentation and query examples. No executable code, network calls, or filesystem access. The skill provides AI guidance for generating database queries. All 116 static findings are false positives from SQL syntax and markdown formatting misidentification.
valueStatement: Writing efficient database queries requires deep knowledge of SQL and NoSQL patterns. This skill generates optimized queries with proper parameterization and explains execution strategies for better performance.
limitations: Does not connect to actual databases or execute queries
Cannot access your database schema without you providing it
Does not provide real-time performance monitoring

useCases.title: Write complex JOIN queries
useCases.description: Generate multi-table joins with proper filtering and aggregation for reporting features

useCases.title: Create aggregation pipelines
useCases.description: Build MongoDB aggregation pipelines and window functions for analytics and data transformation

useCases.title: Generate ORM queries
useCases.description: Create type-safe database queries using Prisma, TypeORM, or Mongoose with proper relations

promptTemplates.title: Basic query request
promptTemplates.prompt: Get all active users with their email addresses created in the last 30 days

promptTemplates.title: Join queries
promptTemplates.prompt: Get users with their orders including order items and product details for completed orders

promptTemplates.title: ORM queries
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a Prisma query to find top 10 customers by total spending with their order counts

promptTemplates.title: Optimization review
promptTemplates.prompt: Optimize this query: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE YEAR(created_at) = 2024 AND status = 'active'

bestPractices: Always use parameterized queries with placeholders like $1 or ? instead of string interpolation
Specify only the columns you need instead of using SELECT *
Add appropriate indexes on columns used in WHERE, JOIN, and ORDER BY clauses
antiPatterns: Using string concatenation to build queries - this creates SQL injection vulnerabilities
Executing queries without LIMIT in production - always paginate large result sets
Using functions on indexed columns in WHERE clauses - this prevents index usage

faq.question: Which databases are supported?
faq.answer: Supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, SQL Server, MongoDB, DynamoDB, Redis, and Cassandra with their respective query syntax.

faq.question: What ORMs work with this skill?
faq.answer: Prisma, TypeORM, Sequelize, SQLAlchemy, Django ORM, and Mongoose are all supported with example patterns.

faq.question: Does this skill execute queries?
faq.answer: No. This skill generates query examples. You must copy and run the queries in your own database environment.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill only generates text - it cannot access your databases, credentials, or any system resources.

faq.question: How do I get better query suggestions?
faq.answer: Provide your table names, columns, and relationships. Include any specific filters or sorting requirements for optimal results.

faq.question: How is this different from raw SQL generation?
faq.answer: The skill provides both raw SQL and ORM alternatives. ORM versions integrate directly with your code using your existing models.
